hook: Jerusalem Post and CBC World — opposite political universes — both said the same thing about Israeli buffer zone in southern Lebanon. Nobody else did.

tweets.text: 11 buckets, 17 articles, 4 distinct frames in today's coverage.
Frame: CEASEFIRE_VIOLATION_PATTERN (2 buckets). "Israel strikes Beirut suburbs for 1st time since ceasefire was announced" — CBC World

Frame: HEZBOLLAH_CONTINUED_OPERATIONS (1 bucket). "Two soldiers were moderately wounded, and an additional soldier was severely wounded in two separate incidents involving explosive drones l…" — Jerusalem Post
Frame: CIVILIAN_CASUALTIES_ONGOING (1 bucket). "Israeli airstrike kills 4 and injures 33 in southern Lebanon despite fragile ceasefire" — AfricaNews
Frame: STATE_SOVEREIGNTY_STRUGGLE (1 bucket). "Why disarming Hezbollah is about much more than guns and rockets" — CBC World
The paradox: Israel pursues military elimination; Lebanon pursues political disarmament—both using the zone, but for contradictory ends. (Jerusalem Post & CBC World, opposite blocs.)
**Canada - CBC World** (canada): Lebanon government pivots toward disarming Hezbollah and asserting state sovereignty post-ceasefire.
